title: PalLLM Install Manifest
description: Single file written to runtime-root/install-manifest.json by scripts/install-mod.ps1. Records every filesystem touchpoint a PalLLM install creates so scripts/uninstall-mod.ps1 can undo it precisely without guessing. Pattern follows 2026 transactional-install best practice (Helm-style atomic install with rollback). Source of truth: scripts/PalLLM.InstallManifest.ps1.

SchemaVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Always 1 for now. The reader rejects manifests with a different version so a v2 manifest can never be silently mis-uninstalled by an older script.

InstallId.description: Unique installation identifier. Useful for correlating manifests with audit logs.

InstalledAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when this install completed.

Producer.description: Script that wrote this manifest. Always 'install-mod.ps1' today.

ProducerVersion.description: Version stamp of the producer (sidecar exe FileVersion when packaged, 'dev' otherwise).

ModRoot.description: Mod root the PalLLM mod was installed under (Palworld's UE4SS Mods directory). Never deleted on uninstall - it belongs to Palworld / UE4SS.

InstalledModPath.description: Absolute path of the installed PalLLM mod directory (or junction).

SourcePath.description: Absolute path of the source the mod was copied from (the repo's mod directory or the release zip's mod payload).

InstallMode.description: How the mod was installed. 'Junction' keeps the mod live-editable from the repo source; 'Copy' is the default for end users.

Artifacts.description: Every filesystem touchpoint the install created, in the order it created them. Uninstall walks this in reverse to undo precisely.

Kind.description: What kind of filesystem object this entry tracks. Uninstall picks the right removal verb per kind (Remove-Item -Recurse for 'directory', plain Remove-Item for 'junction' so it doesn't recurse into the source).

Source.description: Where the artifact was copied from (for traceability). Null for synthesized files like enabled.txt.

AddedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when this artifact was created. Used by rollback to walk artifacts in reverse-creation order.

Metadata.description: Optional free-form metadata (e.g. file size, sha256, source-version).
